context: Tests whether the agent follows RSpec best practices for time-dependent model specs: using travel_to/freeze_time instead of hardcoded past dates, present-tense example names with no 'should' or 'and', correct let/let! discipline, and preferring build over create for factory usage.

checklist.name: Frozen string pragma
checklist.description: The spec file begins with `# frozen_string_literal: true` as its very first line

checklist.name: Full constant path describe
checklist.description: Uses `RSpec.describe Subscription` (constant reference, not a string)

checklist.name: travel_to or freeze_time usage
checklist.description: Uses `travel_to` or `freeze_time` to control time in at least one time-dependent example (trial?, expired?, days_remaining, active?)

checklist.name: No hardcoded past dates
checklist.description: Does NOT use expressions like `X.days.ago` or `Time.now - X` as static record attributes to simulate time passage — instead uses time travel helpers

checklist.name: No 'and' in examples
checklist.description: No it/specify description string contains the word 'and' (case-insensitive, word boundary)

checklist.name: context 'when' naming
checklist.description: Uses `context 'when ...'` or `context 'with ...'` for scenario variations (e.g. before/after expiry, within/outside trial window)

checklist.name: Prefer build over create
checklist.description: Factory calls use `build` or `build_stubbed` rather than `create` for at least one subscription record where persistence is not required

checklist.name: let not let! for non-prerequisite data
checklist.description: Subscription test data is defined with `let` (lazy), not `let!`, unless there is a clear reason the object must exist before the test body runs

checklist.name: Method-level describe blocks
checklist.description: Each predicate method (trial?, expired?, days_remaining, active?) has its own `describe '#method?'` block
